revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Fuso-kyo]][[ja:扶桑教]]
{{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320105121|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}
{{notability|date=March 2023}}
{{Infobox organization | Name = Fusōkyō (扶桑教) | image = File:Fusokyo Headquarters.JPG | image size = 280 | Caption = Fusōkyō Headquarters (Taishi) ({{ill|Setagaya|en|Setagaya|qid=Q231645|lt=Setagaya, Tokyo|1=Setagaya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) | Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Headquarters (Taishi) ({{ill|Setagaya|en|Setagaya|qid=Q231645|lt=Setagaya, Tokyo|1=Setagaya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}})|coord={{coord|35|40|6.9|N|139|39|9.6|E}}}} | Map2 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Original Shrine ({{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi|1=Fujiyoshida}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}})|coord={{coord|35|28|22.9|N|138|47|38.5|E}}}} | Established = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Type = {{ill|Religious corporation (Japan)|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious corporation (Japan)|1=religious corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7311343}} | Headquarters = ** '''Headquarters (Taishi):''' {{ill|Setagaya|en|Setagaya|qid=Q231645|lt=Setagaya, Tokyo|1=Setagaya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, 1-7-20 Matsubara ** '''Original Shrine:''' {{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi|1=Fujiyoshida}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, 1 Kamiyoshida | Founder = {{ill|Shishino Nakaba|ja|宍野半|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishino Nakaba|1=Shishino Nakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536362}} | Leader = Kancho (Head Priest) {{ill|Shishino Fumio|ja|宍野史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishino Fumio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Website = [http://www.fusokyo.org/ Fusōkyō Official Website] }}
{{nihongo|'''Fuso-kyo'''|扶桑教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref>{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=ja:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref> [[Oomoto]] used to be registered as a subgroup of it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Shinto Sects |url=http://www.philtar.ac.uk/encyclopedia/shinto/sects.html |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=www.philtar.ac.uk}}</ref> Shinto-Tokumitsu-kyo was also registered as a subgroup of it<ref>{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yA3_11C6pPgC&dq=%22fuso-kyo%22&pg=PA212 |title=Essentials of Shinto: An Analytical Guide to Principal Teachings |date=1994 |publisher=Greenwood Publishing Group |isbn=978-0-313-26431-3 |language=en}}</ref>
It is considered a "{{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|qid=Q93697439|lt=Mountain worship|1=Mountain worship}} sect" alongside [[Jikkō kyō]]<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yA3_11C6pPgC&dq=Fuso-kyo&pg=PA212 |title=Essentials of Shinto: An Analytical Guide to Principal Teachings |date=1994 |publisher=Greenwood Publishing Group |isbn=978-0-313-26431-3 |language=en}}</ref> and {{ill|Ontake-kyō|en|Ontake-kyō|qid=Q6880734|lt=Ontakekyō|1=Ontake-kyō}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=What is sectarian Shinto? - The Handy Religion Answer Book |url=https://www.papertrell.com/apps/preview/The-Handy-Religion-Answer-Book/Handy%20Answer%20book/What-is-sectarian-Shinto/001137024/content/SC/52cb017682fad14abfa5c2e0_default.html |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=www.papertrell.com}}</ref><ref name=":1" />
{{ill|Hasegawa Kakugyo|en|Hasegawa Kakugyo|qid=Q11630860|lt=Hasegawa Kakugyo|1=Hasegawa Kakugyo}} (who was also associated with [[Jikkō kyō]]) is traditionally seen as having founded it<ref>{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=xlG3F6gSm9UC&dq=Jikk%C5%8D+ky%C5%8D&pg=PA131 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |isbn=978-0-8108-7372-8 |language=en}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Fusōkyō''' (扶桑教) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shinto|en|Sect Shinto|qid=Q1532150|lt=Shintō-derived new religions|1=Sect Shinto}}, originating from the '''Fuji Issan Kōsha''' (富士一山講社), a federation of {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|qid=Q11456201|lt=Fuji-kō|1=Fujiko (religion)}} groups established in '''1873 (Meiji 6)'''. It is considered a successor to the lineage of '''Miroku-ha Fuji-dō''' (身禄派富士道), tracing its origins to {{ill|Kakugyō|ja|角行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakugyō|1=bishop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1529540}}, the legendary founder of Fuji faith.<ref name="PAGE">{{cite news |url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/580dbe6272da2d4dd0a34a3d63c3e571336b19bb |title=Fuji and Religion (6): From Edo-period Fuji-kō to Religious Corporation – Revival of Fujizuka and Shintō Fusōkyō |publisher=THE PAGE |date=2018-01-05 |accessdate=2018-11-04 }}</ref>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shishino Nakaba|ja|宍野半|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishino Nakaba|1=Shishino Nakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536362}}, the first '''officially appointed''' chief priest of '''{{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|en|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|qid=Q653180|lt=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}}''', as well as the priest of '''{{ill|Kitaguchi Hongū Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|北口本宮冨士浅間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaguchi Hongū Fuji Sengen Shrine|qq=}}''', began organizing Fuji-kō practitioners into a unified federation in '''1873 (Meiji 6)'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=菅田正昭|title=古神道は甦る|publisher=たま出版|year=1985|isbn=4884811321}}</ref> On '''September 9''', the federation was officially established as '''Fuji Issan Kōsha''', primarily incorporating the Miroku-ha sect that ascended Mount Fuji from the '''Yoshida Trail'''. The organization also aimed to integrate various Fuji-kō sects from other climbing routes, such as '''Subashiri Trail''', '''Ōmiya Trail''', and '''Murayama Trail'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=井上順孝|title=教派神道の形成|publisher=弘文堂|year=1991|isbn=978-4335160219}}</ref>
In '''1875 (Meiji 8)''', the organization was renamed '''Fuji Issan Kyōkai'''. The following year, in '''1876 (Meiji 9)''', a '''Taishi (Great Shrine)''' was established, and the organization was officially renamed '''Fusōkyō'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=井上順孝|title=教派神道の形成|publisher=弘文堂|year=1991|isbn=978-4335160219}}</ref>
In '''1882 (Meiji 15)''', Fusōkyō gained official recognition as one of the '''Thirteen Sect Shintō Organizations''' (教派神道) and published its scripture, '''"Fusōkyō"'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=井上順孝|title=教派神道の形成|publisher=弘文堂|year=1991|isbn=978-4335160219}}</ref>
== Successive Leaders == {{See also|Fuji-kō#Notable leaders}} * '''Founder:''' '''{{ill|Kakugyō|ja|角行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakugyō|1=bishop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1529540}}''' (藤原角行東覚) – Founder of Fuji-dō * '''Reformer:''' '''{{ill|Jikigyō Miroku|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikigyō Miroku|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}}''' (伊藤食行身禄) – Founder of Miroku-ha Fuji-dō * '''First Kancho:''' '''{{ill|Shishino Nakaba|ja|宍野半|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishino Nakaba|1=Shishino Nakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536362}}''' – Fuji-dō '''Seventh Generation''' * '''Second Kancho:''' '''Shishino Takemaru''' (宍野健丸) – Fuji-dō '''Eighth Generation''' * '''Third Kancho:''' '''Shishino Kenichi''' (宍野健弌) – Fuji-dō '''Ninth Generation''' * '''Fourth Kancho:''' '''Shishino Takeyuki''' (宍野健之) – Fuji-dō '''Tenth Generation''' * '''Fifth Kancho:''' '''Sugiyama Ichitarō''' (杉山一太郎) – Fuji-dō '''Eleventh Generation''' * '''Sixth Kancho:''' '''Shishino Fumio''' (宍野史生) – Fuji-dō '''Twelfth Generation'''<ref name="PAGE" />
== Locations ==
=== '''Taishi (Headquarters)''' === * '''Location:''' '''1-7-20 Matsubara, Setagaya, Tokyo''' * The headquarters of Fusōkyō, originally located in '''Shiba, Tokyo''' (now '''Hamamatsuchō, Minato, Tokyo'''). * In '''2018''', the '''Matsubara Fujizuka''' (松原富士), which was destroyed in WWII, was reconstructed after '''73 years'''.<ref name="PAGE" />
=== '''Genshi (Original Shrine)''' === * '''Location:''' '''1 Kamiyoshida, Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi''' * Located next to '''{{ill|Kitaguchi Hongū Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|北口本宮冨士浅間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaguchi Hongū Fuji Sengen Shrine|qq=}}''', the founding site of Fusōkyō.
=== '''Fujisan Tenpai-gū (富士山天拝宮)''' === * '''Location:''' '''Yoshida Trail, 8th Station, Mount Fuji''' * Originally located '''near the Fuji Summit (金明水)''', the shrine was relocated to its current location due to the '''Meiji-period Shinbutsu Bunri (separation of Shintō and Buddhism)'''.
=== '''Eboshiiwa Shrine (烏帽子岩神社)''' === * '''Location:''' '''Yoshida Trail, 8th Station, Mount Fuji''' * Located behind '''Fujisan Tenpai-gū''', marking the spot where '''Jikigyō Miroku''' practiced fasting and attained enlightenment.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|qid=Q11456201|lt=Fuji-kō|1=Fujiko (religion)}} * {{ill|Jikkōkyō|ja|実行教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikkōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Jikkō kyō|qid=Q11452837}} * {{ill|Maruyama Kyō|ja|丸山教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kyō|1=Maruyamakyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368215}} * {{ill|Tenmukyō|ja|天母教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmukyō|1=Tenmo Religion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11273531}} * {{ill|Kakugyō|ja|角行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakugyō|1=bishop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1529540}} * {{ill|Jikigyō Miroku|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikigyō Miroku|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}}
== External Links == * [http://www.fusokyo.org/ Fusōkyō Official Website] * [{{NDLDC|816036}} 'Fujishindōki' (1877) by Shishino Nakaba] – A primer on Fuji faith.
== See Also == * [[Fujiko (religion)]] * [[Jikkō kyō]]
== References == <references />

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!-- Note: The following pages were redirects to [[Fuso-kyo]] before draftification: * --> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Fuso-kyo]] {{wikidata link|Q11497295}} [[Category:13 Shinto Sects]] [[Category:Fuji worship]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinto denominations]] [[Category:Shinto new religious movements]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
